steps.prompt: Which word is the finite verb?

hints.Homo: 'Homo' is a noun meaning 'man/person' — the subject
hints.cenam: 'Cenam' is a noun meaning 'supper/dinner' in the accusative
hints.magnam: 'Magnam' is an adjective meaning 'great' in the accusative feminine

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'facit' — 'fecit' has the perfect stem 'fec-'
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'fecerat' — 'fecit' is simple perfect

hints.fio, fieri, factus sum, —: These are the parts of 'fieri' (to become) — the passive of 'facere'
hints.fugio, fugere, fugi, fugitum: These are the parts of 'fugere' (to flee)
hints.fallo, fallere, fefelli, falsum: These are the parts of 'fallere' (to deceive)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'hominem' — 'Homo' is the nominative subject
hints.vocative: Vocative could be 'homo' but here it is the narrative subject, not an address

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'hominis' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'cena' — the -am ending marks accusative
hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'cena' — 'cenam' with -am is accusative

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'cenae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'cenae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'cenae' ends in -ae

translation: A certain man made a great supper
